title: Share of children achieving minimum reading proficiency by the end of lower-secondary age
caption:
Share of children achieving minimum reading proficiency by the end of lower-secondary age

Percentage of children at lower secondary school age meeting reading comprehension standards. This data accounts for school completion rates to estimate reading skills for all children, not just those in school.

This bar chart displays the share of children achieving minimum reading proficiency by the end of lower-secondary age in 2022, arranged in descending order from left to right. The vertical axis measures the percentage from 0% to 80%, while the bars are color-coded by income level: dark purple for low-income, light purple for lower-middle-income, light green for upper-middle-income, and dark green for high-income countries. Ireland leads the chart with the highest share, exceeding 80%, followed closely by Australia, Lithuania, and Spain, all of which are high-income countries represented by dark green bars. These top-performing nations cluster near the upper limit of the chart. Moving toward the center, Turkey appears as a light green bar, followed by Greece and Moldova, which sit in the mid-range between 50% and 60%. Further down the ranking, Brazil, Kazakhstan, and Azerbaijan appear as light green bars in the 30% to 40% range. The right side of the chart shows a distinct shift in color and value, dominated by light purple and dark purple bars. The Dominican Republic and Cambodia, both lower-middle-income countries shown in light purple, fall near the bottom, with shares dropping below 20%. The visual distribution highlights a gradient where high-income countries predominantly occupy the left side with higher percentages, while lower-income and lower-middle-income countries cluster on the right with significantly lower proficiency shares.
